title: Boundary unkeyed-features sidecar
description: Sibling sidecar (`<file>.geojson.unkeyed.json`) for an LGD-keyed boundary GeoJSON, listing features that were dropped from the emitted file because they could not be joined to the LGD registry. Surfaces a denominator so the UI can say "47 of 12,524 villages (0.4%) lack an LGD join" rather than silently shrink the dataset (defuses Hans's Negativity-instinct concern). Per TODO/TN-GRANULAR-GEO-PLAN.md Phase 0.

x-changelog.description: Initial schema. Records dropped features (unkeyed against LGD registry) plus totals so the citizen-facing UI can show retention rate. Top-level: $schema, $schema_version, for, dropped (array of {source_feature_name, reason, dropped_at}), totals ({original, retained, dropped}). Empty `dropped` array with totals.dropped=0 is the canonical "perfect join" signal.

Major: add required top-level `sources` array (CLAUDE.md §12). The unkeyed sidecar is itself a published artifact under datasets/, so it carries its own provenance — typically the same upstream URL(s) the parent GeoJSON cites, copied at emit time so a frontend reading only the sidecar can attribute the dropped-feature counts. Bumped same day as v1.0; no v1.0 data files were ever committed beyond the bundle this fixup commit also corrects.

for.description: Basename of the sibling GeoJSON this sidecar describes (e.g. "S22-villages-603.geojson").

sources.description: CLAUDE.md §12 provenance — typically copied verbatim from the parent GeoJSON's `<file>.geojson.sources.json` so the sidecar is independently attributable.

original.description: Feature count in the upstream source before LGD-join filtering.

retained.description: Feature count in the emitted GeoJSON. MUST equal original - dropped.

dropped.description: Feature count dropped for lack of LGD join. MUST equal len(dropped[]).
One entry per feature that could not be matched to the LGD registry. Empty array with totals.dropped=0 is a perfect join.

source_feature_name.description: Name of the feature in the upstream source as best we can identify it (often the only handle we have when the LGD code is missing).

reason.description: Why the feature was dropped. `other` requires a non-empty `notes`.

dropped_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp when the emitter dropped this feature.
